content: Liver, red meat, and shellfish are rich in zinc and copper, essential minerals.
Eating 10 ounces of red meat a day provides less than a tenth of the daily requirement for iron.
Eating liver once a week can provide a significant amount of nutrients, including iron, zinc, and copper.
Cutting out legumes and vegetables can lead to deficiencies in folate and polyphenols, among other nutrients.
Phytates in natural foods can inhibit iron absorption, but adding phytate-rich foods like legumes and vegetables is recommended.
Calcium supplementation can reduce iron absorption, but should be done in moderation to avoid excess calcium intake.
Donating blood is the first-line defense for managing iron status, as 90% of body iron is in red blood cells.
